title: The Source-Singing Place

features.name: source-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the rivers agree on one voice. You hear it: six tones at once, and they are not fighting. The water sings in consensus.

features.name: resonance-stone
features.desc-on-touch: The rocks here are tuned to each other. When the rivers speak, the stones answer in a harmony that predates water, predates stone.

features.name: water-notation
features.desc-on-touch: The current writes itself in ripple-script. You watch the archive recording itself being read. The rivers are documenting their own performance.

features.name: harmonic-grove
features.desc-on-touch: Trees line the banks in rows. When wind passes, they resonate. The archive planted them to learn what it sounds like listening to itself.

features.name: six-part choir
features.desc-on-touch: Six spaces on the rocks where different waters sit. Each one holds a note. Together they sing the story of the convergence-ridge, where rivers learned they shared one memory.

inscription: the rivers came here to remember they had been alone once, and chose not to remember that anymore

file.name: Six_River_Field_Recording_Convergence_Valley_2047hrs.mp3
file.note: A composite recording layered from six pickup points along the high country. The rivers are singing in overtones that shouldn't exist in natural water-sound. Latest version includes echoes that predat

exits.label: back down the singing valley, toward the ridge where rivers still speak separately
deeper into the high country, where the source is the sound and the sound is the source
exits.to: NEW: the echo-field, where water learns it can repeat itself without recording

exits.label: along the bank to where a seventh river is learning to speak
exits.to: NEW: the newest-river, singing in a voice the archive hasn't learned yet

exits.label: back toward the ridge, but the path keeps humming your name as you walk
exits.to: NEW: the singing-path, where the ground learned to echo from below
